description:
Advances: mutagen.mutation_pipeline

Deliverable:
1. Complete `priv/helper_scripts/bench_ast_perf.exs`. The harness should:
   - Run against the wrd25_200sites fixture (committed in S1)
   - Measure wall-clock per-site time and total run time
   - Measure peak memory usage (`:erlang.memory/0` snapshots)
   - Report a before/after comparison (the user runs it once before merging S1, captures the number, then again after S5 lands)
   - Record SHA-256 of the produced NDJSON output to document byte-identity
2. New `test/mutagen_ex/memory_test.exs` (tagged `:e2e_slow`) that generates a 1000-site synthetic fixture programmatically (NOT committed to disk) and runs MutagenEx.run/2 on it. Asserts the run completes without OOM. NO heap size assertion (too brittle); just "does not crash with `:emfile` or similar".
3. CHANGELOG entry for `.25` recording: (a) the measured speedup number from the bench harness, (b) the SHA-256s of NDJSON before/after to evidence byte-identity, (c) one line per feature shipped (Ast lift, AstCache extension, dispatch table, batched prewalk, BeamCache).
4. README revision: if the README has a perf section, update with current numbers. Otherwise add a one-line caveat removal (no more "Known caveat: per-site prewalk is expensive").

NOTE: If the measured speedup is < 2x (the spec lower bound for "2-4x speedup"), DO NOT close this ticket. Instead:
   a. Triage in the bw ticket comments: was the bench fixture representative? Did Mutators.normalize/1 (out of scope per `.25`) dominate?
   b. Open a new follow-up `mutagen-wrd.25-fu2` capturing the gap.
   c. Document findings in CHANGELOG anyway — partial speedup is still a win.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: `mix test` green; `mix test --include e2e_slow test/mutagen_ex/memory_test.exs` green; bench harness runs without error and produces a number
- Behaviors to demonstrate: bench harness produces a documented speedup number; CHANGELOG records the before/after SHA-256s; memory test passes on a 1000-site synthetic fixture

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/**/* (no production code changes — all benefits already landed in S1-S5)
Allowed touches:
- priv/helper_scripts/bench_ast_perf.exs (complete)
- priv/helper_scripts/bench_fixtures/wrd25_200sites/** (only if minor fixture tuning needed)
- test/mutagen_ex/memory_test.exs (new)
- CHANGELOG.md
- README.md

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do NOT modify any production code in `lib/`. If the bench reveals a perf bug, open a follow-up; do not fix in this stage.
- Do NOT commit the 1000-site fixture to disk. Memory test generates it at runtime (with a fixed seed for determinism).
- Memory test asserts only "did not crash", NOT specific heap sizes. Heap-size assertions are flaky across BEAM versions.

Merge gate:
- [ ] `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` green
- [ ] `mix test` green
- [ ] Memory test green (`:e2e_slow` tag)
- [ ] Bench harness runs to completion and produces a numbered speedup
- [ ] CHANGELOG records the speedup number AND the before/after NDJSON SHA-256s
- [ ] If speedup < 2x: a follow-up ticket exists AND CHANGELOG explains
- [ ] No code changes in lib/ (grep verification)
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

Commit: `mutagen-wrd.25.06: bench harness + memory test + .25 CHANGELOG`
title: S6: bench harness + memory test + .25 CHANGELOG
